summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of the legendary May 15 1970 Fillmore East show – superb performance with smoking Dark Star and The Other One.
review: The archive’s SBD is virtually pristine, delivering a tight, bright mix that lets every acoustic nuance and electric jam shine.  Musically this night is a highlight of ’70, featuring a soaring Dark Star, a thunder‑rolling The Other One, and standout folk‑rock gems like Friend of the Devil, Candyman and I Know You Rider.  The occasional tiny glitch (a brief tape splice on China Cat Sunflower) does not detract from the overall brilliance.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 50 detailed reviews overwhelmingly praise both performance and sound quality; only minor, isolated criticisms appear.  The weighted blend of show (70 %) and recording (30 %) yields a near‑top rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear, tight mix with studio‑like fidelity; occasional hiss or brief tape splice on a couple of tracks but overall superb and very listenable.

poor_songs: St. Stephen (early set – a bit strained)
show_quality.setlist_flow: Two acoustic sets book‑end a long electric stretch, with New Riders of the Purple Sage inserted; reviewers note seamless transitions, lively banter and an overall pacing that keeps the energy high from start to finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar tone is described as perfect, his pedal‑steel on NRPS is “unreal,” and his vocals are praised even when a lyric flub occurs.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lesh’s bass work gets special kudos for rolling thunder on The Other One and holding down the low end throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s rhythm guitar and vocal banter are noted as amusing and integral to the show’s vibe.
band_member_comments.Keys: Pigpen’s organ is called “tapestry” and Keith (or Brent) receives praise for solid keyboard support on both acoustic and electric material.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s percussion shines, especially on Dark Star; his drum solo in the late set gets positive mentions.
